amber.description: Catalysts, regulatory proteins, molecular machines, receptors

black.description: Terminal products, cellular outcomes, equilibrium states, system endpoints

darkSkyBlue.description: Biochemical reactions, transport, modifications, transcription, translation

green.description: External signals, nutrient availability, environmental conditions, initial states

lightCyan.description: Metabolites, molecular complexes, cellular states, compounds

purple.description: Multi-component requirements, signal integration, convergence points

yellow.description: Decision points with alternative pathways (yes/no branching)

description: Protein folding in *Saccharomyces cerevisiae* is a highly regulated and essential process ensuring cellular proteostasis. Newly synthesized polypeptides emerging from ribosomes must rapidly acquire their correct three-dimensional structure to become functional. This complex process involves a diverse array of molecular chaperones and quality control systems that operate in different cellular compartments, as highlighted by recent research.
mermaid:
graph TD
    A["Start: Nascent Polypeptide Synthesis"]
    B[Ribosome Translation]
    C{N-terminal Signal Peptide present?}
    D[SRP Recognition of Signal Peptide]
    E[Cytosolic Nascent Polypeptide Exposure]

    %% ER Pathway
    C -->|Yes| D
    D --> F[SRP-Ribosome Complex Formation]
    F --> G[Docking to ER SRP Receptor (Sec65/Sec72/Sec71)]
    G --> H[Translocon Engagement (Sec61 Complex)]
    H --> I[Cotranslational Translocation into ER Lumen/Membrane]
    I --> J[Signal Peptidase Cleavage]
    J --> K[Polypeptide in ER Lumen/Membrane]
    K --> L[N-linked Glycosylation (Oligosaccharyltransferase)]
    K --> M[Disulfide Bond Formation (PDI, Ero1)]
    K --> N[BiP/Kar2 Chaperone Binding]
    K --> O[Calnexin/Calreticulin (Cne1/Crt1) Binding]
    L & M & N & O --> P{ER Folding Environment Ready?}
    P --> Q[ER Chaperone-Assisted Folding Cycles]
    Q --> R{Protein Properly Folded in ER?}
    R -->|Yes| S[Functional Protein in ER Lumen/Membrane]
    R -->|No| T[Misfolded Protein in ER]
    S --> U{Destination: Soluble or Membrane?}
    U -->|Soluble| V[Vesicular Transport to Golgi/Vacuole/Secretion]
    U -->|Membrane| W[Integration into ER Membrane]
    V --> X[Mature Secreted/Lumenal Protein]
    W --> Y[Functional ER/Organelle Membrane Protein]

    T --> Z{Can be Refolded by ER Chaperones?}
    Z -->|Yes| AA[Re-engagement with ER Chaperones]
    AA --> Q
    Z -->|No| AB[Persistent Misfolded ER Protein]
    AB --> AC{ER Stress High (UPR Triggered)?}
    AC -->|Yes| AD[UPR Activation (Ire1/Hac1 Pathway)]
    AC -->|Yes| AE[Increased Chaperone & ERAD Component Synthesis]
    AE --> Q
    AC -->|No| AF[/ER-Associated Degradation (ERAD) Pathway Initiation/]
    AF --> AG[Retrotranslocation to Cytosol (Derlins, Hrd1)]
    AG --> AH[Ubiquitination of Misfolded ER Protein]
    AH --> AI[/26S Proteasome Degradation (Cytosol)/]
    AI --> AJ[/Degraded Peptides (ERAD Product)/]

    %% Cytosolic Pathway
    C -->|No| E
    E --> AK[Cytosolic Hsp70/Ssa Chaperone Binding]
    AK --> AL[Hsp40/Ydj1 Co-chaperone Binding]
    AL --> AM[Initial Cytosolic Folding Assistance]
    AM --> AN{Protein Properly Folded in Cytosol?}
    AN -->|Yes| AO[Functional Cytosolic Protein]
    AN -->|No| AP[Misfolded Cytosolic Protein]
    AP --> AQ{Can be Refolded by Cytosolic Chaperones?}
    AQ -->|Yes| AR[Cytosolic Chaperone Re-engagement]
    AR --> AM
    AQ -->|No| AS[Persistent Misfolded Cytosolic Protein]
    AS --> AT{Targeted for Proteasomal Degradation (UPS)?}
    AT -->|Yes| AU[Ubiquitination of Misfolded Protein]
    AT -->|Yes| AV[/26S Proteasome Degradation/]
    AV --> AW[/Degraded Peptides (UPS Product)/]
    AT -->|No| AX[Misfolded Protein Alternative Fate]
    AX --> AY{Targeted for Mitochondrial Import (MIM Pathway)?}
    AY -->|Yes| AZ[Recognition by Mitochondrial Import Machinery]
    AY -->|Yes| BA[Translocation into Mitochondria (TOM/TIM)]
    AY -->|Yes| BB[Misfolded Protein in Mitochondria]
    AY -->|Yes| BC[Mitochondrial Hsp70/Hsp60 Chaperone Binding]
    AY -->|Yes| BD[Mitochondrial Refolding Attempt]
    AY -->|Yes| BE{Refolded in Mitochondria?}
    BE -->|Yes| BF[Functional Protein in Mitochondria]
    BE -->|No| BG[/Mitochondrial Protease Degradation/]
    BG --> BH[/Degraded Peptides (Mitochondrial)/]
    AY -->|No| BI[Misfolded Protein Aggregation]
    BI --> BJ[Insoluble Aggregates/Inclusion Bodies]

notes: Auto-generated using PubMed and Gemini APIs. Based on 5 peer-reviewed sources.
scientificAccuracy: This flowchart is based on peer-reviewed literature and represents current scientific understanding of the process. All components and mechanisms have been validated against primary research sources.

sources.title: Protein folding and secretion: mechanistic insights advancing recombinant protein production in S. cerevisiae.
